Container.Name: BabyMonitor
Container.Overview:
Monitors audio from an RTSP camera stream and sends alerts to Home Assistant when noise exceeds a configurable threshold. Designed for baby monitoring but works with any RTSP camera.
Uses FFmpeg ebur128 filter for accurate volume measurement. Publishes live volume to MQTT and fires a Home Assistant webhook when noise is detected. Features dual camera fallback on connection loss, auto-reconnect, and configurable threshold + trigger count to avoid false positives.
No code changes needed — fully configured via environment variables.

Config.Name: Primary Camera URL
Config.Description: Primary RTSP stream URL — e.g. rtsp://user:pass@192.168.1.x:554/stream

Config.Name: Fallback Camera URL
Config.Description: Secondary RTSP URL — used automatically when the primary fails (optional)

Config.Name: Noise Threshold (dBFS)
Config.Description: Volume threshold in dBFS. Higher = more sensitive. Silent room: -60 to -45. Baby crying: -20 to -10. Start at -30 and adjust.

Config.Name: Trigger Count
Config.Description: Consecutive loud readings required before alert fires — avoids false positives from brief sounds

Config.Name: MQTT Topic
Config.Description: Topic for live volume readings (used for MQTT sensor in HA)

Config.Name: HA Webhook URL
Config.Description: Home Assistant webhook URL to call when noise is detected

Config.Name: Retry Delay (s)
Config.Description: Seconds to wait before reconnecting after camera connection loss
